title: Online Election
description:
You are given two integer arrays persons and times. In an election, the ith vote was cast for persons[i] at time times[i].

For each query at a time t, find the person that was leading the election at time t. Votes cast at time t will count towards our query. In the case of a tie, the most recent vote (among tied candidates) wins.

Implement the TopVotedCandidate class:

*   TopVotedCandidate(int[] persons, int[] times) Initializes the object with the persons and times arrays.
*   int q(int t) Returns the number of the person that was leading the election at time t according to the mentioned rules.

solutionCode.python:
```python
def profitableSchemes(n, minProfit, group, profit):
    dp = [[0] * (minProfit + 1) for _ in range(n + 1)]
    mod = 10**9 + 7
    dp[0][0] = 1
    for i in range(len(group)):
        for j in range(n, group[i] - 1, -1):
            for k in range(minProfit, -1, -1):
                dp[j][k] = (dp[j][k] + dp[j - group[i]][max(0, k - profit[i])]) % mod
    return sum(dp[i][minProfit] for i in range(1, n + 1)) % mod
```
    
    We can use dynamic programming to solve this problem. In this case, we will create a 2D array dp[i][j] to store the number of schemes for i members and at least j profit. We initialize dp[0][0] = 1, because there is one scheme with 0 members and 0 profit: the empty set.

We then loop through each crime, and for each crime, we loop through the number of members from n to group[i], and the minimum profit from minProfit to 0. Then, we update dp[j][k], which represents j members and k profit. The updated value will be the sum of the current value and the value for `(j-group[i])` members and the max between 0 and `k-profit[i]`, which represents the possible combinations of less members required for the current crime plus the profit generated by the current crime. The value is taken modulo mod to keep the result withing bounds.

Finally, we sum the values of dp[i][minProfit] for i from 1 to n and return the result modulo mod. This will give us the total number of profitable schemes that can be chosen.
